q: Mind precedes all things; mind is their chief, mind made are they. If with a pure mind one speaks or acts, happiness follows like a never-departing shadow.
plain: Your mind shapes your life. Act from a clear, kind mind and peace follows you.

q: Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world. By non-hatred alone is hatred appeased. This is an eternal law.
plain: You cannot end anger with more anger — only kindness truly heals it.

q: Just as a solid rock is not shaken by the storm, even so the wise are not affected by praise or blame.
plain: Stay steady within, whether others praise you or criticize you.

q: He who conquers himself is greater than one who conquers a thousand men in battle a thousand times.
plain: The hardest and most worthwhile victory is mastery over your own mind.

q: As a bee gathers nectar and departs without harming the flower or its colour or scent, so let the sage move through the village.
plain: Take only what you need, and leave the world unharmed and a little brighter.

q: Drop by drop is the water pot filled. Likewise, the wise one, gathering it little by little, fills oneself with good.
plain: Small good deeds, repeated daily, add up to a good life.

q: Health is the greatest gift, contentment the greatest wealth, a trusted friend the best relative, Nibbāna the highest bliss.
plain: True riches are health, a content heart, good friends, and inner peace.

q: You yourself must strive. The Buddhas only point the way.
plain: Teachers can show the path, but the walking is yours to do.

q: Conquer anger with non-anger. Conquer evil with good. Conquer the miser with giving. Conquer the liar with truth.
plain: Answer each fault with its opposite virtue, not with the same fault.

q: Mindfulness is the path to the Deathless; heedlessness is the path to death. The mindful do not die; the heedless are as if already dead.
plain: Living with awareness is truly living; living on autopilot is a kind of sleep.

q: Better than a thousand useless words is one useful word, hearing which one attains peace.
plain: A single wise, kind word is worth more than endless empty talk.

q: All conditioned things are impermanent. When one sees this with wisdom, one turns away from suffering.
plain: Everything changes. Seeing this clearly loosens our grip on what causes us pain.

q: Irrigators guide the water; fletchers shape the arrow; carpenters bend the wood; the wise master themselves.
plain: Just as craftspeople shape their material, the wise patiently shape their own minds.

q: Whatever a mother or father might do for a beloved child, a rightly-directed mind can do for you even better.
plain: A well-trained mind is your greatest protector and friend.

q: Let one not despise what one has received, nor envy others. The monk who envies others does not attain peace of mind.
plain: Contentment with what you have is the doorway to a calm heart.

q: There is no fire like passion, no grip like hatred, no snare like delusion, no torrent like craving.
plain: Our deepest troubles begin in the mind — in craving, anger, and confusion.

q: Should a person do good, let them do it again and again. Let them find joy in it, for blissful is the accumulation of good.
plain: Do good not once but as a habit, and delight in the doing.

q: Speak the truth; do not give way to anger; give of the little you have to one who asks. By these three steps you draw near the gods.
plain: Honesty, patience, and generosity quietly lift a whole life.

q: One who holds back arisen anger as one checks a rolling chariot — that one I call a true charioteer.
plain: Real strength is the calm that reins in anger before it runs away with you.

q: Even as a mother protects with her life her only child, so let one cultivate a boundless heart toward all beings.
plain: Extend the same tender care you give a loved one to every living being.

q: Let none deceive another, nor despise any being in any state. Let none through anger or ill-will wish harm upon another.
plain: Wish no harm on anyone — this is the heart of loving-kindness.

q: Long is the night to the wakeful; long is the road to the weary; long is saṃsāra to the foolish who know not the true Dhamma.
plain: Without understanding, life feels endlessly heavy; wisdom makes the journey lighter.

q: By oneself is evil done; by oneself is one defiled. By oneself is evil left undone; by oneself is one purified.
plain: No one else can purify your heart — that work is yours, and it is possible.

q: The gift of Dhamma surpasses all gifts; the taste of Dhamma surpasses all tastes; the joy of Dhamma surpasses all joys.
plain: Sharing truth and wisdom is the most lasting gift we can give.
